<!--
  Architecture Decision Record (ADR).
  Copy to docs/adr/ADR-NNN-{slug}.md.
  Lightweight Michael Nygard format with minor additions for this team.

  ADRs capture *why* a decision was made — the context, the alternatives,
  the consequences. They don't expire; superseded ADRs are kept and linked.
-->

---
id: ADR-NNN
title: <decision in one line>
status: proposed                  # proposed | accepted | superseded by ADR-MMM | deprecated
date: <YYYY-MM-DD>
deciders: <names or roles>
related-spec: <F-{AREA}-{NNN} if any>
related-story: <#NNN if any>
---

# ADR-NNN — <Title>

## Context

The forces in tension. Why this decision is being made *now*. Constraints (regulatory, technical, organisational) that bound the choice. Stakeholders affected.

Two to four paragraphs.

## Decision

**We will <choice>.**

One paragraph. Specific. The decision itself, not the deliberation.

## Alternatives considered

For each meaningful alternative we evaluated:

### Alternative A — <name>
- **What it is:** ...
- **Why we considered it:** ...
- **Why we rejected it:** ...

### Alternative B — <name>
- **What it is:** ...
- **Why we considered it:** ...
- **Why we rejected it:** ...

(Add or remove as needed. If only one alternative was meaningfully considered, the decision is suspect — either the alternatives weren't really alternatives, or we're rationalising a default. Surface this honestly.)

## Consequences

What this decision makes easier, harder, or impossible going forward.

### Positive
- ...

### Negative / risks
- ...

### What we'll need to do that we wouldn't have otherwise
- ...

### What we no longer need to worry about
- ...

## Open questions

Things still unresolved at decision time. Each gets an owner and a date.

| Question | Owner | Due |
|----------|-------|-----|
| <question> | <name> | <YYYY-MM-DD> |

## Revision history

| Rev | Date | Author | Change |
|-----|------|--------|--------|
| 1   | <YYYY-MM-DD> | <name> | Initial draft |
